整套首页
unit_05 0% 正确率 0 复习 0 错题

unit_05

推荐练习默认不提示本单元词。需要复盘时可手动显示本单元词；提交后判分，也可放弃查看答案。

50 推荐题 284 全部题目 0 弱相关题 30% 推荐答案命中比例
#1 Text Completion
Some species camouflage patterns may be ______, granting those species a higher chance of survival in a heterogeneous environment.Some species camouflage patterns may be ______, granting those species a higher chance of survival in a heterogeneous environment.
答案：A　plastic
patternheterogeneous
pattern: standard unit headword | heterogeneous: standard unit headword
#2 Sentence Equivalence
The company is so old-fashioned and opposed to innovation that it can seem downright ______.The company is so old-fashioned and opposed to innovation that it can seem downright ______.
答案：A, C　antediluvian; archaic
patronizeantediluvian
patronize: standard unit headword | antediluvian: standard unit headword
#3 SE
The climate researchers' __________ method of presentation caused a stir among those who felt that scientific papers should be dispassionate in their delivery of evidence.The climate researchers' __________ method of presentation caused a stir among those who felt that scientific papers should be dispassionate in their delivery of evidence.
答案：A, C　prejudicial; tendentious
causedispassionate
cause: standard unit headword | dispassionate: standard unit headword
#4 TC TRIPLE
Computers triumphed at chess not by (i) __________ human thought, but by playing like machines. The analogy with flight is (ii) __________: as long as people tried to fly by imitating birds, they were doomed to failure; once they (iii) __________ the paradigm of the familiar, however, they were soon flying much faster than birds.Computers triumphed at chess not by (i) __________ human thought, but by playing like machines. The analogy with flight is (ii) __________: as long as people tried to fly by imitating birds, they were doomed to failure; once they (iii) __________ the paradigm of the familiar, however, they were soon flying much faster than birds.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, F, H　aping; instructive; escaped
doomparadigm
doom: standard unit headword | paradigm: standard unit headword

Mr. Billington, at times, can be a (i) . For instance, he deplores the Royal Shakespeare Company's eight-and-a-half-hour version of Nicholas Nickel by, which many found (ii) .Mr. Billington, at times, can be a (i) . For instance, he deplores the Royal Shakespeare Company's eight-and-a-half-hour version of Nicholas Nickel by, which many found (ii) .
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, F　naysayer; absorbing
braggartpushover
braggart: standard unit headword | pushover: standard unit headword
#6 Text Completion
Though many professional book reviewers would agree that criticism should be (i)______ enterprise, a tendency to write (ii)______ reviews has risen, partly out of the mistaken belief that sharing personal details will help reviewers stand out of the pack.Though many professional book reviewers would agree that criticism should be (i)______ enterprise, a tendency to write (ii)______ reviews has risen, partly out of the mistaken belief that sharing personal details will help reviewers stand out of the pack.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, E　an anonymous; confessional
confessionalanonymous
confessional: standard unit headword | anonymous: standard unit headword
#7 SE
Aside from nutrient availability, growth rate is the primary determinant of size in single-celled organisms rapidly growing Escherichia coli cells are more than twice as large as their __________ counterparts.Aside from nutrient availability, growth rate is the primary determinant of size in single-celled organisms rapidly growing Escherichia coli cells are more than twice as large as their __________ counterparts.
答案：C, E　more sluggish; slower
versatileadaptable
versatile: standard unit headword | adaptable: standard unit headword
#8 TC TRIPLE
Several times in this crime series, the police chief presumes herself to be (i) __________, only to learn that, on the contrary, her role is that of (ii) __________: it requires her to (iii) __________ the wishes of her political bosses, who want to hide whatever malfeasance has paralyzed the local police department. At first willing to reconcile herself to the imposture, she is eventually impelled to rebel by her innate sense of justice and her formidable skills as a gumshoe.Several times in this crime series, the police chief presumes herself to be (i) __________, only to learn that, on the contrary, her role is that of (ii) __________: it requires her to (iii) __________ the wishes of her political bosses, who want to hide whatever malfeasance has paralyzed the local police department. At first willing to reconcile herself to the imposture, she is eventually impelled to rebel by her innate sense of justice and her formidable skills as a gumshoe.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, E, H　autonomous; a figurehead; conform to
formidablesuspect
formidable: standard unit headword | suspect: standard unit headword
#9 Sentence Equivalence
In noting that critical and popular opinions about Li's art coincided, Chuang ______ the existence of an exception to her general theory of art criticism, which posits that critics' views do not intersect with those of the general public.In noting that critical and popular opinions about Li's art coincided, Chuang ______ the existence of an exception to her general theory of art criticism, which posits that critics' views do not intersect with those of the general public.
答案：B, C　conceded; acknowledged
coincideconcede
coincide: standard unit headword | concede: standard unit headword
#10 Text Completion
Many historians of the ancient world are wary of sounding (i)______. Write so much as a sentence and the temptation is immediately to (ii)______ it. Even in cases when the sources for a given event are (iii)______, uncertainties and discrepancies crop up everywhere.Many historians of the ancient world are wary of sounding (i)______. Write so much as a sentence and the temptation is immediately to (ii)______ it. Even in cases when the sources for a given event are (iii)______, uncertainties and discrepancies crop up everywhere.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, F, H　dogmatic; qualify; plentiful
anachronisticfusty
anachronistic: standard unit headword | fusty: standard unit headword
#11 SE
Although it is premature to say whether nanomotors would __________ conventional methods, Wang believes that the most promising applications for nanomotors lie in fast drug delivery or low-cost cleanup of environmental pollutants.Although it is premature to say whether nanomotors would __________ conventional methods, Wang believes that the most promising applications for nanomotors lie in fast drug delivery or low-cost cleanup of environmental pollutants.
答案：D, E　supplant; supersede
complementpromise
complement: standard unit headword | promise: standard unit headword

Even though the survey course was designated as interdisciplinary, it involved no real ____________ of subject matter.Even though the survey course was designated as interdisciplinary, it involved no real ____________ of subject matter.
答案：B　fusion
fusion
fusion: standard unit headword
#13 Text Completion
Some ethicists worry that a deeper understanding of the brain may be tantamount to ______: if we discover that free will is an illusion of neural circuitry, how will we hold people responsible for their actions?Some ethicists worry that a deeper understanding of the brain may be tantamount to ______: if we discover that free will is an illusion of neural circuitry, how will we hold people responsible for their actions?
答案：E　exculpation
vindicationvalediction
vindication: standard unit headword | valediction: standard unit headword
#14 TC SINGLE
The efficacy of a placebo may not ___________ deception: inert sugar pills have been shown to reduce the symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome even in patients who were explicitly told they were receiving a placebo.The efficacy of a placebo may not ___________ deception: inert sugar pills have been shown to reduce the symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome even in patients who were explicitly told they were receiving a placebo.
答案：C　require
reduce
reduce: standard unit headword

Wild-eyed and boisterous, she sought out the companionship of others, even as her face revealed a great (ii) .As her fortunes diminished and her reputation ebbed, Hopkins' need for (i) increased. Wild-eyed and boisterous, she sought out the companionship of others, even as her face revealed a great (ii) .
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, E　conviviality; anxiety
boisterous

#16 Sentence Equivalence
Architects may be more extroverted and therefore the more ______ member of a bridge design team, but they are not always the most essential.Architects may be more extroverted and therefore the more ______ member of a bridge design team, but they are not always the most essential.
答案：D, F　visible; salient
salient
salient: standard unit headword
#17 TC SINGLE
Needing to conceal their increasing alienation from each other, the family settled into what amounted to voluble nonsense, in that they would ____________ but seldom communicate.Needing to conceal their increasing alienation from each other, the family settled into what amounted to voluble nonsense, in that they would ____________ but seldom communicate.
答案：C　natter
alienation
alienation: standard unit headword

By focusing exclusively on what lawyers can or cannot get away with,core legal ethics courses often inadvertently promote a(ii) ethics, thus creating an(iii) audience for efforts to instill an appropriately broad grasp of the ethical challenges lawyers face.According to Merrin, many law school curricula are(i) the challenge of fostering an' adequate ethical understanding among their students. By focusing exclusively on what lawyers can or cannot get away with,core legal ethics courses often inadvertently promote a(ii) ethics, thus creating an(iii) audience for efforts to instill an appropriately broad grasp of the ethical challenges lawyers face.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, E, I　unequal to; cynical sense of; unfavorable
legal
legal: standard unit headword
#19 TC DOUBLE
By using (i)________ prose throughout, the contributors to this anthology have been able to make their subject seem(ii)________ in the extreme—of concern only to one another.By using (i)________ prose throughout, the contributors to this anthology have been able to make their subject seem(ii)________ in the extreme—of concern only to one another.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, D　esoteric; parochial
parochial
parochial: standard unit headword
#20 Sentence Equivalence
In matters of taste, the art patron and collector Peggy Guggenheim was ______: she was for the strangest, the most surprising, the most satisfying, the best, the unique.In matters of taste, the art patron and collector Peggy Guggenheim was ______: she was for the strangest, the most surprising, the most satisfying, the best, the unique.
答案：D, F　an extremist; a zealot
patron
patron: standard unit headword
#21 SE
When Gustave Flaubert wrote a rabidly depressed letter to his friend George Sand, she responded with a maternal nudge, typical of their correspondence; she liked to pretend that his _________ was an affectation.When Gustave Flaubert wrote a rabidly depressed letter to his friend George Sand, she responded with a maternal nudge, typical of their correspondence; she liked to pretend that his _________ was an affectation.
答案：C, E　abjection; wretchedness
correspondence
correspondence: standard unit headword

Though extremely (i) about her own plans, she allowed her associates no such (ii) and was constantly badgering them for information about what they intended to do nextThough extremely (i) about her own plans, she allowed her associates no such (ii) and was constantly badgering them for information about what they intended to do next
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：C, D　reticent; discretion
discretion
discretion: standard unit headword
#23 TC TRIPLE
Famous books have been(i) the theater: Dickens suffered so much hackneyed staging that he felt forced to change his writing style, throwing time shifts into his narratives to trip up the adapters. Undeterred, theatrical managements ever since have used other people's literary repute to fill seats, though the adaptations still seldom(ii) their literary models. But in the1990s a small British theater company, Shared Experience, began to (iii) the received wisdom about adaptations by developing thrilling and uniquely theatrical versions of several classic novels.Famous books have been(i) the theater: Dickens suffered so much hackneyed staging that he felt forced to change his writing style, throwing time shifts into his narratives to trip up the adapters. Undeterred, theatrical managements ever since have used other people's literary repute to fill seats, though the adaptations still seldom(ii) their literary models. But in the1990s a small British theater company, Shared Experience, began to (iii) the received wisdom about adaptations by developing thrilling and uniquely theatrical versions of several classic novels.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, D, G　much abused by; equaled; confound
theatrical
theatrical: standard unit headword
#24 TC SINGLE
Roi Wagner argues that the __________ nature of the notions of infinity since early modernity precludes their being subsumed under a single metaphor.Roi Wagner argues that the __________ nature of the notions of infinity since early modernity precludes their being subsumed under a single metaphor.
答案：D　variegated
metaphor
metaphor: standard unit headword
#25 Text Completion
The kilt has certainly had (i)______ history, outlawed as it was by Britain in 1746 as an emblem of nationalist subversion, then more recently (ii)______ from men’s to women’s apparel in a 2003 European Union survey, and ridiculed at various times for a wide variety of reasons.The kilt has certainly had (i)______ history, outlawed as it was by Britain in 1746 as an emblem of nationalist subversion, then more recently (ii)______ from men’s to women’s apparel in a 2003 European Union survey, and ridiculed at various times for a wide variety of reasons.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：C, D　a contentious; reclassified
ridicule
ridicule: standard unit headword
#26 Sentence Equivalence
The sociologist argued that criminal behavior is an impermanent condition because it is the result of cyclical forces operating through ______ factors, not the manifestation of deeply rooted personal characteristics.The sociologist argued that criminal behavior is an impermanent condition because it is the result of cyclical forces operating through ______ factors, not the manifestation of deeply rooted personal characteristics.
答案：A, C　contingent; circumstantial
contingent
contingent: standard unit headword
#27 SE
Laboratory experiments testing whether memories can be purposely erased or implanted seem decidedly __________ compared to the sensational depictions of memory manipulation in movies, but such research has nevertheless yielded dramatic results.Laboratory experiments testing whether memories can be purposely erased or implanted seem decidedly __________ compared to the sensational depictions of memory manipulation in movies, but such research has nevertheless yielded dramatic results.
答案：D, F　prosaic; pedestrian
sensational
sensational: standard unit headword

Unlike news reports, theater isn’t expected to stick to the facts. Built on a sandy foundation of make-believe and (i)_______, the form, by nature traffics in (ii)_______. Good documentary drama exploits its inherent paradox: creating artifice from verbatim texts, it uncovers truths by playing on the tension between what’s real and what’s (iii)_______.Unlike news reports, theater isn’t expected to stick to the facts. Built on a sandy foundation of make-believe and (i)_______, the form, by nature traffics in (ii)_______. Good documentary drama exploits its inherent paradox: creating artifice from verbatim texts, it uncovers truths by playing on the tension between what’s real and what’s (iii)_______.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, D, H　pretense; illusion; invented
uncover
uncover: standard unit headword

It was debated. It didn't happen overnight. No one was sure how it would turn out.At its best, the storytelling in this television series manages to accommodate a sense of historical (i) __________. The narrative shows that American independence was not (ii) __________. It was debated. It didn't happen overnight. No one was sure how it would turn out.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, D　contingency; inevitable

答案：E　propitious

(ii)______, the text makes it clear that the author’s examination of these sources has been similarly (iii)______.

Blank 2
Blank 3

答案：B, C　fractious; restive

Orchestra players are not always the best judges of conductors. Not surprisingly, when evaluating conductors they put a high premium on musicianship and (i) __________ and therefore typically gravitate toward conductors who are both technically competent and (ii) __________. But the ability to galvanize a hundred musicians into giving a great performance requires more than proficiency and affability.Orchestra players are not always the best judges of conductors. Not surprisingly, when evaluating conductors they put a high premium on musicianship and (i) __________ and therefore typically gravitate toward conductors who are both technically competent and (ii) __________. But the ability to galvanize a hundred musicians into giving a great performance requires more than proficiency and affability.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, E　collegiality; personally likable
premium
premium: standard unit headword

The book is a determinedly apolitical writer's attempt to deal with an explosive subject, and some readers will think it too __________; they will miss the impassioned engagement found in the works of her peers.The book is a determinedly apolitical writer's attempt to deal with an explosive subject, and some readers will think it too __________; they will miss the impassioned engagement found in the works of her peers.
答案：E　measured
impassioned

答案：A, E　elucidation; demystification

#37 Text Completion
Movement from bottom to top, from poor to rich, was rare, even movement from poor to middle class was (i)______. Statistical analysis of trends in occupation, income, and property ownership, Thernstrom wrote, “yielded rather (ii)______ conclusions about social mobility in nineteenth-century America.” So we might expect Thernstrom to be suspicious now of claims that differences in class could be (iii)______ if only the public schools did a better job.Movement from bottom to top, from poor to rich, was rare, even movement from poor to middle class was (i)______. Statistical analysis of trends in occupation, income, and property ownership, Thernstrom wrote, “yielded rather (ii)______ conclusions about social mobility in nineteenth-century America.” So we might expect Thernstrom to be suspicious now of claims that differences in class could be (iii)______ if only the public schools did a better job.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, E, I　an anomaly; pessimistic; obliterated
suspicious
suspicious: standard unit headword

The laboratory maze has grown ever less ______ since it was invented; instead of hoping to lose a rodent in a labyrinth, today’s scientists design mazes to elicit a few simple, easily measurable behaviors.The laboratory maze has grown ever less ______ since it was invented; instead of hoping to lose a rodent in a labyrinth, today’s scientists design mazes to elicit a few simple, easily measurable behaviors.
答案：A, B　intricate; convoluted
maze
maze: standard unit headword

The idea that Antarctica's subglacial lakes are ____________ has changed radically thanks to repeat-pass satellite altimetry technology, which has found evidence of water transfer between lakes at distances of more than 200 kilometers.The idea that Antarctica's subglacial lakes are ____________ has changed radically thanks to repeat-pass satellite altimetry technology, which has found evidence of water transfer between lakes at distances of more than 200 kilometers.
答案：C, D　isolated; discrete
discrete

答案：A　an autonomous

This is partly because televised courtroom dramas, which compete with soap operas, bring to the screen some of the same elements of family (i) that appeal to soap fans. Blank 2

Blank 2

Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, E, H　an approximation; reconsideration; immediacy

答案：A, F　accession to; acquiescence to

答案：E　maudlin

While Americans are portrayed as (ii) __________ in this version of the story, research reveals that most listeners knew the program was fictional.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：C, E　credulity; gullible

Blank 1
Blank 2

答案：A, B　diminutive; minuscular

答案：E　meretricious
repugnant

答案：E　credibility

In American art the line between the good and the goods is not a hard-and-fast boundary, for in a commercial society the membrane that separates spirit and store is always ______.In American art the line between the good and the goods is not a hard-and-fast boundary, for in a commercial society the membrane that separates spirit and store is always ______.
答案：A, C　porous; permeable
hard-and-fast
hard-and-fast: standard unit headword
#56 Text Completion
The experimental theater company’s members know that their performances (i)______ an audience, that they were dense and unpredictable and not always easy to digest. But none of the techniques used would be (ii)______ anyone with an interest in music or films. Indeed, they would seem strange only to people who expected to see traditionally crafted plays. The actors therefore felt that theater critics’ derisive commentary showed only that the critics (iii)______ the company’s work.The experimental theater company’s members know that their performances (i)______ an audience, that they were dense and unpredictable and not always easy to digest. But none of the techniques used would be (ii)______ anyone with an interest in music or films. Indeed, they would seem strange only to people who expected to see traditionally crafted plays. The actors therefore felt that theater critics’ derisive commentary showed only that the critics (iii)______ the company’s work.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, E, I　made demands on; alien to; misunderstood
alien
alien: standard unit headword

Whereas water on Mars could plausibly have been delivered by repeated comet impacts in the geologically recent past, the ice on smaller bodies is more difficult to attribute to __________ source.Whereas water on Mars could plausibly have been delivered by repeated comet impacts in the geologically recent past, the ice on smaller bodies is more difficult to attribute to __________ source.
答案：A　an exogenous

答案：C, D　gratify; please

Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, D　umbrage; collectively overthrow

答案：C　inscrutable

答案：A, B　superficial; sketchy

Women in the mining towns of the American West were strictly stereotyped into neat categories of public and private, good and bad, but the 100 intrepid female prospectors in Zanjani’s book managed to (i)______ those categories. In addition to providing documentation that demolishes the all-male version of prospecting, Zanjani uses the examples of her female loners to (ii)______ some of the (iii)______ generalizations about Euro-American women as uniformly nurturant and sociable pioneers.Women in the mining towns of the American West were strictly stereotyped into neat categories of public and private, good and bad, but the 100 intrepid female prospectors in Zanjani’s book managed to (i)______ those categories. In addition to providing documentation that demolishes the all-male version of prospecting, Zanjani uses the examples of her female loners to (ii)______ some of the (iii)______ generalizations about Euro-American women as uniformly nurturant and sociable pioneers.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, H　confound; puncture; facile

They teach the reader that a struggle against severe difficulties in life is (i)______, that it is an intrinsic part of human existence, and that if one does not (ii)______, but steadfastly meets unexpected and often unjust hardships, one masters all obstacles and at the end (iii)______.Many fairy tales are complex narratives of wish fulfillment. They teach the reader that a struggle against severe difficulties in life is (i)______, that it is an intrinsic part of human existence, and that if one does not (ii)______, but steadfastly meets unexpected and often unjust hardships, one masters all obstacles and at the end (iii)______.

答案：B, F, H　unavoidable; shy away; emerges victorious

答案：C　numinous

#70 Text Completion
For Ruskin, architecture serves the community only when approached in a spirit of piety and (i)______ . Architecture must set effective boundaries to public space, and it does so by (ii)______ the desire to show off, to stand out, to record the artistic flair of some temporary ego. Architecture succeeds in its public task through (iii)______ and devotion, of the kind that can be observed in the moulding, firing and laying of a properly proportioned brick, but which is violated at every point by Frank Gehry’s bombastic Guggenheim Museum in Bilbao.For Ruskin, architecture serves the community only when approached in a spirit of piety and (i)______ . Architecture must set effective boundaries to public space, and it does so by (ii)______ the desire to show off, to stand out, to record the artistic flair of some temporary ego. Architecture succeeds in its public task through (iii)______ and devotion, of the kind that can be observed in the moulding, firing and laying of a properly proportioned brick, but which is violated at every point by Frank Gehry’s bombastic Guggenheim Museum in Bilbao.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, F, G　sacrifice; relinquishing; humility

Blank 2
答案：C, D　mercurial; inconstancy

答案：C　profligate
profligate
profligate: standard unit headword
#107 TC DOUBLE
Faced with a drastic loss of revenue, executives of local newspapers belong to two strategic camps: the (i)_______ and the (ii)_______. The former, trying to do more of the same but more cheaply, are doomed. Only the latter, embracing a fundamentally different way of doing business, will prosper in the face of unstoppable change.Faced with a drastic loss of revenue, executives of local newspapers belong to two strategic camps: the (i)_______ and the (ii)_______. The former, trying to do more of the same but more cheaply, are doomed. Only the latter, embracing a fundamentally different way of doing business, will prosper in the face of unstoppable change.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, D　economizers; visionaries
doom
doom: standard unit headword
#108 TC TRIPLE
For scientists who wish to conduct research in the national parks, the new plan promises a warm welcome in place of the (i)_______, or even resistance, that often greets requests for research permits. The plan says that “parks can and should be centers for broad scientific research and inquiry.” It also maintains that scientific research should be (ii)_______ in parks where it can be performed without (iii)_______ other park values.For scientists who wish to conduct research in the national parks, the new plan promises a warm welcome in place of the (i)_______, or even resistance, that often greets requests for research permits. The plan says that “parks can and should be centers for broad scientific research and inquiry.” It also maintains that scientific research should be (ii)_______ in parks where it can be performed without (iii)_______ other park values.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, F, H　indifference; facilitated; jeopardizing
promise
promise: standard unit headword
#109 Sentence Equivalence
An apparent paradox led the scientists to pursue their present line of research: they were struck by the fact that a single mathematical formula can be used to describe physical phenomena that appear to be so ______.An apparent paradox led the scientists to pursue their present line of research: they were struck by the fact that a single mathematical formula can be used to describe physical phenomena that appear to be so ______.
答案：E, F　heterogeneous; dissimilar
heterogeneous
heterogeneous: standard unit headword
#110 TC SINGLE
Increased dopamine is positively correlated to impulsivity; if the connection is causal, then higher levels of melatonin from exposure to more darkness at night should __________ impulsive behaviors, since higher levels of melatonin cause dopamine to drop.Increased dopamine is positively correlated to impulsivity; if the connection is causal, then higher levels of melatonin from exposure to more darkness at night should __________ impulsive behaviors, since higher levels of melatonin cause dopamine to drop.
答案：D　inhibit
cause
cause: standard unit headword
#111 SE
When Gustave Flaubert wrote a rabidly depressed letter to his friend George Sand, she responded with a maternal nudge, typical of their correspondence; she liked to pretend that his was an affectation.When Gustave Flaubert wrote a rabidly depressed letter to his friend George Sand, she responded with a maternal nudge, typical of their correspondence; she liked to pretend that his was an affectation.
答案：C, E　abjection; wretchedness
correspondence
correspondence: standard unit headword
#112 Sentence Equivalence
Edited collections of scholarly essays generally tend to be somewhat uneven: they suffer from the ______ subject matter of the various essays, the lack of an overarching and consistent thesis, and the variable quantity of the contributions.Edited collections of scholarly essays generally tend to be somewhat uneven: they suffer from the ______ subject matter of the various essays, the lack of an overarching and consistent thesis, and the variable quantity of the contributions.
答案：B, C　disparate; heterogeneous
heterogeneous
heterogeneous: standard unit headword
#113 TC DOUBLE
It is hard to know exactly what is causing a worldwide proliferation of jellyfish, but we can speculate. The fishing industry has (i)_______ populations of jellyfish predators, such as red tuna, swordfish, and sea turtles. And when small, plankton-eating fish, such as anchovies, are (ii)_______, jellyfish flourish, gorging on plankton and reproducing prolifically.It is hard to know exactly what is causing a worldwide proliferation of jellyfish, but we can speculate. The fishing industry has (i)_______ populations of jellyfish predators, such as red tuna, swordfish, and sea turtles. And when small, plankton-eating fish, such as anchovies, are (ii)_______, jellyfish flourish, gorging on plankton and reproducing prolifically.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, F　depleted; overharvested
cause
cause: standard unit headword
#114 TC TRIPLE
Anna Barbauld's 1804 edition of Richardson's correspondence has been (i) __________. Although she (ii) __________ many of the letters that she included and made stylistic changes throughout, often for the worse, she was, by the standards of her time, quite (iii) __________ editor with regard to her respect for the original texts.Anna Barbauld's 1804 edition of Richardson's correspondence has been (i) __________. Although she (ii) __________ many of the letters that she included and made stylistic changes throughout, often for the worse, she was, by the standards of her time, quite (iii) __________ editor with regard to her respect for the original texts.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, F, G　unjustly disparaged; abridged; a scrupulous
correspondence
correspondence: standard unit headword
#115 TC SINGLE
While so-called bioplastics have the potential to significantly reduce our dependence on fossil fuels, they're not __________: claims about bioplastics being biodegradable and compostable are greatly exaggerated.While so-called bioplastics have the potential to significantly reduce our dependence on fossil fuels, they're not __________: claims about bioplastics being biodegradable and compostable are greatly exaggerated.
答案：B　a panacea
reduce
reduce: standard unit headword
#116 SE
Attempts to calm down the _________ toddler proved futile: he fidgeted, hollered, and whined during the entire three-hour plane flight.Attempts to calm down the _________ toddler proved futile: he fidgeted, hollered, and whined during the entire three-hour plane flight.
答案：B, F　recalcitrant; restive
restive
restive: standard unit headword
#117 TC TRIPLE
Letters present an awkward stumbling block to those who take a high line about literature as a custodian of moral values. The letters of accomplished poets and novelists are - as often as not - about drinking and infidelity, about sponging and wasting time. Consequently, one's (i) __________ the writer as (ii) __________ can often come in for a rude shock on contemplation of the writer's correspondence: the soul that composed the poems and the self that wrote the letters can seem (iii) __________.Letters present an awkward stumbling block to those who take a high line about literature as a custodian of moral values. The letters of accomplished poets and novelists are - as often as not - about drinking and infidelity, about sponging and wasting time. Consequently, one's (i) __________ the writer as (ii) __________ can often come in for a rude shock on contemplation of the writer's correspondence: the soul that composed the poems and the self that wrote the letters can seem (iii) __________.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, E, G　reverence for; exemplary; irreconcilable
correspondence
correspondence: standard unit headword
#118 TC DOUBLE
Where there has been extensive natural or human-caused disturbance, pines (i) __________ the landscape because they colonize soon after disturbance, grow rapidly, and (ii) __________ drought, cool temperatures, and nutrient-poor soils.Where there has been extensive natural or human-caused disturbance, pines (i) __________ the landscape because they colonize soon after disturbance, grow rapidly, and (ii) __________ drought, cool temperatures, and nutrient-poor soils.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：C, F　can achieve dominance throughout; endure
cause
cause: standard unit headword
#119 Sentence Equivalence
A few decades ago the idea of animal morality would have been met with ______; however，recent research suggests that animals not only act altruistically but also have the capacity for empathy, forgiveness, trust, and reciprocity.A few decades ago the idea of animal morality would have been met with ______; however，recent research suggests that animals not only act altruistically but also have the capacity for empathy, forgiveness, trust, and reciprocity.
答案：A, E　derision; ridicule
ridicule
ridicule: standard unit headword
#120 SE
The reliable production of usable energy through the controlled fusion of hydrogen atoms into helium remains an elusive dream: even the most ardent efforts to achieve it have thus far been __________.The reliable production of usable energy through the controlled fusion of hydrogen atoms into helium remains an elusive dream: even the most ardent efforts to achieve it have thus far been __________.
答案：C, F　thwarted; frustrated
fusion
fusion: standard unit headword
#121 TC SINGLE
The country's highest court is hardly known for its __________, leaving citizens with little hope of a quick ruling on recent legal challenges.The country's highest court is hardly known for its __________, leaving citizens with little hope of a quick ruling on recent legal challenges.
答案：A　alacrity
legal
legal: standard unit headword

答案：A, F　fiery; impassioned
impassioned
impassioned: standard unit headword

Williams concludes that Gale's embrace of literary (i) __________ accounts for her eventual decline in reputation. Gale sought professional affiliation with other women writers of many stripes, and Williams contends that she has therefore been (ii) __________ by a later literary paradigm that favors (iii) __________ as a marker of artistic prowess.Why Zona Gale - a Pulitzer Prize-winning writer whose novels were both a critical and popular success - has fallen so far out of the literary canon is the fascinating subject of Deborah Lindsay Williams' recent book. Williams concludes that Gale's embrace of literary (i) __________ accounts for her eventual decline in reputation. Gale sought professional affiliation with other women writers of many stripes, and Williams contends that she has therefore been (ii) __________ by a later literary paradigm that favors (iii) __________ as a marker of artistic prowess.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, I　sisterhood; marginalized; individual exceptionalism
paradigm
paradigm: standard unit headword

Undeterred, theatrical managements ever since have used other people's literary repute to fill seats, though the adaptations still seldom (ii) __________ their literary models. But in the 1990s a small British theater company, Shared Experience, began to (iii) __________ the received wisdom about adaptations by developing thrilling and uniquely theatrical versions of several classic novels.Famous books have been (i) __________ the theater: Dickens suffered so much hackneyed staging that he felt forced to change his writing style, throwing time shifts into his narratives to trip up the adapters. Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, D, G　much abused by; equaled; confound

#131 TC TRIPLE
While many tools can automatically locate patterns in large data sets, they cannot yet distinguish truly significant patterns from those that are (i) __________. Thus statisticians, who by virtue of their training are (ii) __________ identifying (iii) __________ interpretations of patterns in data, must increasingly bear the burden of scrutinizing data and challenging conclusions based on statistical flukes.While many tools can automatically locate patterns in large data sets, they cannot yet distinguish truly significant patterns from those that are (i) __________. Thus statisticians, who by virtue of their training are (ii) __________ identifying (iii) __________ interpretations of patterns in data, must increasingly bear the burden of scrutinizing data and challenging conclusions based on statistical flukes.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, E, G　merely fortuitous; adept at; spurious
pattern
pattern: standard unit headword
#132 SE
Nanocrystalline photocatalysts are __________ as removers of hazardous contaminants; thus, expanding their practical applicability by improving their visible light absorption is one of the most important objectives in photocatalytic studies.Nanocrystalline photocatalysts are __________ as removers of hazardous contaminants; thus, expanding their practical applicability by improving their visible light absorption is one of the most important objectives in photocatalytic studies.
答案：B, F　promising; viable
promise
promise: standard unit headword

After peaking around 3.5 million years ago, the number of large mammalian carnivore species declined gradually over the next million and a half years or so, mostly because the rate at which new species (i) ____________ slowed down while the extinction rate (ii) ____________.After peaking around 3.5 million years ago, the number of large mammalian carnivore species declined gradually over the next million and a half years or so, mostly because the rate at which new species (i) ____________ slowed down while the extinction rate (ii) ____________.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, E　originated; remained largely unchanged

答案：D　terminal

The concept of increasing complexity of organisms has ______ history among evolutionary biologists, and yet many laypeople would unhesitatingly say that the pattern applies to the history of life on Earth.The concept of increasing complexity of organisms has ______ history among evolutionary biologists, and yet many laypeople would unhesitatingly say that the pattern applies to the history of life on Earth.
答案：D, F　a contentious; a fraught

For many years, Americans have had a love affair with ferryboats. Ferries are said to relieve our frayed nerves after we’ve stewed in bumper-to-bumper traffic, and conventional wisdom also says ferries (i)______ congestion and air pollution by getting us out of our cars. Unfortunately, this (ii)______ notion recently has (iii)______ several West Coast mayors, who have in consequence eagerly pursued the implementation of ferry service in their cities.For many years, Americans have had a love affair with ferryboats. Ferries are said to relieve our frayed nerves after we’ve stewed in bumper-to-bumper traffic, and conventional wisdom also says ferries (i)______ congestion and air pollution by getting us out of our cars. Unfortunately, this (ii)______ notion recently has (iii)______ several West Coast mayors, who have in consequence eagerly pursued the implementation of ferry service in their cities.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, E, G　reduce; misguided; captivated
reduce

There is little question that in primates there is (i)________ between monogamous mating partnerships and extensive male care of infants. Nevertheless, that does not mean male care is (ii)________ situations in which females mate with more than one male; studies of savanna baboons show that male care certainly can occur in nonmonogamous systems.There is little question that in primates there is (i)________ between monogamous mating partnerships and extensive male care of infants. Nevertheless, that does not mean male care is (ii)________ situations in which females mate with more than one male; studies of savanna baboons show that male care certainly can occur in nonmonogamous systems.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, D　an association; precluded by
contingent

Many people today blame science and technology for contributing to environmental degradation and to global warming. Indeed, one of the noteworthy features of our time is that the relentless (i) of the power of science is paralleled by a growing sense of (ii) about science.Anyone old enough to recall the public's enthusiasm for so many of the scientific breakthroughs in the 1950s and 1960s will be struck by the more (iii) even fearful, attitude toward scientific developments at present.Many people today blame science and technology for contributing to environmental degradation and to global warming. Indeed, one of the noteworthy features of our time is that the relentless (i) of the power of science is paralleled by a growing sense of (ii) about science.Anyone old enough to recall the public's enthusiasm for so many of the scientific breakthroughs in the 1950s and 1960s will be struck by the more (iii) even fearful, attitude toward scientific developments at present.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, D, I　expansion; distrust; grudging

When a critic peremptorily announces that Titian's canvases are the greatest ever painted or that Mondrian and Malevich are the only truly radical artists of the twentieth century, we enjoy seeing all the confusions of taste swept aside, even though we may (ii) reservations about the (iii) nature of these opinions.There is something seductive about (i) judgments in the arts. When a critic peremptorily announces that Titian's canvases are the greatest ever painted or that Mondrian and Malevich are the only truly radical artists of the twentieth century, we enjoy seeing all the confusions of taste swept aside, even though we may (ii) reservations about the (iii) nature of these opinions.

#159 Text Completion
China’s rapidly growing population is the main threat facing large carnivores in the People’s Republic. Increasingly, policies aimed at limiting population growth have been (i)______; nevertheless, the country’s vast size and the isolation of many its regions mean that human populations in areas where large carnivores still occur (ii)______. This human pressure has (iii)______ the South China tiger.China’s rapidly growing population is the main threat facing large carnivores in the People’s Republic. Increasingly, policies aimed at limiting population growth have been (i)______; nevertheless, the country’s vast size and the isolation of many its regions mean that human populations in areas where large carnivores still occur (ii)______. This human pressure has (iii)______ the South China tiger.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, E, H　implemented; can grow unchecked; doomed
doom
doom: standard unit headword
#160 SE
While most Centaurs (minor planets) are thought to be __________, astronomers have observed patterns of brightening from the Centaur Chiron, as well as activity similar to that of a streaking comet.While most Centaurs (minor planets) are thought to be __________, astronomers have observed patterns of brightening from the Centaur Chiron, as well as activity similar to that of a streaking comet.
答案：B, F　quiescent; dormant
pattern
pattern: standard unit headword

Wind pollination tends to be __________ in thick forests in part because the abundant vegetation blocks air movement.Wind pollination tends to be __________ in thick forests in part because the abundant vegetation blocks air movement.
答案：A　uncommon
versatile
versatile: standard unit headword
#162 Text Completion
The irony of digital networking is that it can produce more (i)______ than did the geographical confinement it supposedly transcended. As human interactions become (ii)______ physical location, people are less likely to have regular dealings with others who do not share the same values and outlooks.The irony of digital networking is that it can produce more (i)______ than did the geographical confinement it supposedly transcended. As human interactions become (ii)______ physical location, people are less likely to have regular dealings with others who do not share the same values and outlooks.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, E　provincialism; less contingent on
contingent
contingent: standard unit headword
#163 TC DOUBLE
A long-running philosophical debate in the field of stratigraphy-the study of sequences of sedimentary rocks-is whether the sedimentary record represents mainly (i) __________ happenings (e.g., waves and tides) or their opposite, (ii) __________ events (e.g., hurricanes and flash floods).A long-running philosophical debate in the field of stratigraphy-the study of sequences of sedimentary rocks-is whether the sedimentary record represents mainly (i) __________ happenings (e.g., waves and tides) or their opposite, (ii) __________ events (e.g., hurricanes and flash floods).
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, E　everyday; extraordinary

discrete: standard unit headword
#164 TC TRIPLE
Models of the interior evolution of Venus suggest that the planet (i) __________ periods of relative (ii) __________ and periods of instability and rapid surface overturn. In other words, Venus may "freak out" occasionally, getting rid of its internal heat in great planetwide (iii) __________ of activity, rather than in the steadier cycling of lithospheric plates that occurs on Earth.Models of the interior evolution of Venus suggest that the planet (i) __________ periods of relative (ii) __________ and periods of instability and rapid surface overturn. In other words, Venus may "freak out" occasionally, getting rid of its internal heat in great planetwide (iii) __________ of activity, rather than in the steadier cycling of lithospheric plates that occurs on Earth.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, F, H　oscillates between; quiescence; paroxysms
pattern
pattern: standard unit headword

An abundance of nutrient-rich pollution in estuaries causes algae to ______, much as houseplants grow better when their soil contains added fertilizer.An abundance of nutrient-rich pollution in estuaries causes algae to ______, much as houseplants grow better when their soil contains added fertilizer.
答案：A, B　abound; proliferate
cause
cause: standard unit headword

答案：E　promise

答案：B, E　exiguous; tenuous
reduce
reduce: standard unit headword
#172 Text Completion
One of the great misconceptions regarding Earth is that it is (i)______ . This is true only if your sense of connection depends on large chunks of physical matter moving from place to place. The dust and rocks that rain down from space are indeed (ii)______ , even if some of the larger rocks are occasionally a (iii)______ . Matter trickles in, trickles out. But matter is not everything: an unending spate of pure luminous energy pours down on Earth in a 170,000-trillion-watt torrent.One of the great misconceptions regarding Earth is that it is (i)______ . This is true only if your sense of connection depends on large chunks of physical matter moving from place to place. The dust and rocks that rain down from space are indeed (ii)______ , even if some of the larger rocks are occasionally a (iii)______ . Matter trickles in, trickles out. But matter is not everything: an unending spate of pure luminous energy pours down on Earth in a 170,000-trillion-watt torrent.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, D, H　isolated; negligible; cause for concern
cause
cause: standard unit headword
#173 SE
Karin Knorr-Cetina argues that the production of technological knowledge is fundamentally __________: technological innovations arise within an "epistemic culture," that is, practitioners bonded by affinity who create and warrant emerging ideas.Karin Knorr-Cetina argues that the production of technological knowledge is fundamentally __________: technological innovations arise within an "epistemic culture," that is, practitioners bonded by affinity who create and warrant emerging ideas.
答案：B, F　collective; social

答案：A, F　unassuming; modest

答案：B, D　apogee; acme

答案：B, D　artificial; spurious

答案：A, E　a magnanimous; a generous

In this single volume, Kenny aims to survey for the general reader all of ancient philosophy. Understandably, space in such a book is (i)______ and he is not to be faulted for minor omissions. However, Kenny would have added significantly to his book’s value had he more effectively (ii)______ the influence of ancient philosophy on the subsequent tradition. As it is, newcomers to the subject will have little (iii)______ the afterlife enjoyed by ancient philosophy in the period 1600-1750.In this single volume, Kenny aims to survey for the general reader all of ancient philosophy. Understandably, space in such a book is (i)______ and he is not to be faulted for minor omissions. However, Kenny would have added significantly to his book’s value had he more effectively (ii)______ the influence of ancient philosophy on the subsequent tradition. As it is, newcomers to the subject will have little (iii)______ the afterlife enjoyed by ancient philosophy in the period 1600-1750.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, E, G　at a premium; signaled; sense of

It may not seem like a big deal for produce distributors to mix together soybeans from different farms, but a growing number of buyers are willing to pay a premium for soybeans the ______ of which is known: they like to know exactly where their food came from.It may not seem like a big deal for produce distributors to mix together soybeans from different farms, but a growing number of buyers are willing to pay a premium for soybeans the ______ of which is known: they like to know exactly where their food came from.
答案：D, F　origin; provenance

答案：C, F　restorative; tonic

答案：B, E　functional; utilitarian

答案：B, D　audacious; striking

The description of humans as having an internal clock is not a (i)______. Or rather, it is–you do not have a tiny watch in your cerebellum–but it also refers to (ii)______, a specialized bundle of cells that regulates cyclical processes.The description of humans as having an internal clock is not a (i)______. Or rather, it is–you do not have a tiny watch in your cerebellum–but it also refers to (ii)______, a specialized bundle of cells that regulates cyclical processes.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：C, F　metaphor; a real biological feature

答案：B, F　enthralled by; dazzled by

答案：B, C　stave off; forestall

#189 Text Completion
To label Hamilton a foreigner because he was born outside what later became the United States is to assume anachronistically the existence of the nation before the fact. It is true that Hamilton came to believe that he was a (i)______ the United States, but his (ii)______ was stemmed not from his (iii)______ but from his confrontation with American democracy, which he considered to be a disease afflicting the nation.To label Hamilton a foreigner because he was born outside what later became the United States is to assume anachronistically the existence of the nation before the fact. It is true that Hamilton came to believe that he was a (i)______ the United States, but his (ii)______ was stemmed not from his (iii)______ but from his confrontation with American democracy, which he considered to be a disease afflicting the nation.

Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, D, I　stranger in; alienation; birthplace

答案：B, E　eclipse; outdo

答案：A, B　tantamount to; synonymous with

答案：A　specious

It is not just the entry of new collectors into the field that is causing this intensification. Established collectors’ interests are also becoming more (ii)______. Those who once concentrated on the work of either the nineteenth-century pioneers or the twentieth-century modernists are now keen to have (iii)______ collections.Now that photographic prints have become a popular field for collecting, auctions are becoming more (i)______. It is not just the entry of new collectors into the field that is causing this intensification. Established collectors’ interests are also becoming more (ii)______. Those who once concentrated on the work of either the nineteenth-century pioneers or the twentieth-century modernists are now keen to have (iii)______ collections.

Blank 2
Blank 3

But once the paradigm (ii)______, the academic incentives shift in the opposite direction: research results are more likely to be considered worthy of publication when they (iii)______ what has become the established view.When a new scientific model emerges, research studies (i)______ that paradigm tend to dominate in the scientific literature: the process of selecting articles for publication is tilted toward positive results. Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, E, I　affirming; has become entrenched; undermine

#212 Text Completion
Human-caused disturbances, such as habitat destruction and the introduction of nonnative species, are among the leading causes of plant and animal population declines. Most populations are affected by a combination of adverse human pressures, each of which is in itself insufficient to (i)______ a population crash. Therefore, studies of population decline that (ii)______ individual factors and thus (iii)______ potential interactions may lead to improper management of declining species.Human-caused disturbances, such as habitat destruction and the introduction of nonnative species, are among the leading causes of plant and animal population declines. Most populations are affected by a combination of adverse human pressures, each of which is in itself insufficient to (i)______ a population crash. Therefore, studies of population decline that (ii)______ individual factors and thus (iii)______ potential interactions may lead to improper management of declining species.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, E, H　trigger; focus on; overlook

答案：B, E　waning; ebbing

vindication: standard unit headword
#214 Text Completion
While people complain about their hectic lives and demanding schedules, one might be justified in suspecting that they are being somewhat (i)______: compulsive busyness seems to be, for many, a source of (ii)______.While people complain about their hectic lives and demanding schedules, one might be justified in suspecting that they are being somewhat (i)______: compulsive busyness seems to be, for many, a source of (ii)______.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, D　disingenuous; pride

答案：B, D　analogous; comparable

Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, D　persistent; corruption

vindication: standard unit headword
#218 Text Completion
To pay for the extra spending under this international poverty plan, each American would have to contribute less than the cost of buying a premium cup of coffee once a week. But financial aid is not (i)______, and even if the funding recommended here were to (ii)______, the grandest objectives may well remain unfulfilled. Nonetheless, carefully targeted aid can reward responsible governments, (iii)______ individual initiative, and alleviate suffering. Many will think that’s worth a cup of coffee.To pay for the extra spending under this international poverty plan, each American would have to contribute less than the cost of buying a premium cup of coffee once a week. But financial aid is not (i)______, and even if the funding recommended here were to (ii)______, the grandest objectives may well remain unfulfilled. Nonetheless, carefully targeted aid can reward responsible governments, (iii)______ individual initiative, and alleviate suffering. Many will think that’s worth a cup of coffee.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, F, I　a panacea; materialize; encourage

premium: standard unit headword
#219 Text Completion
With the emergence of scientific history-writing in the late nineteenth century, several authors sought to ignore the glowing myths surrounding George Washington and uncover the human being within, but their biographies were still (i)______ enough that Washington remained a marbled and remote figure. Indeed, by the 1920s Washington had become such (ii)______ personage that inevitably someone had to go to the other extreme and try to (iii)______ the legend.With the emergence of scientific history-writing in the late nineteenth century, several authors sought to ignore the glowing myths surrounding George Washington and uncover the human being within, but their biographies were still (i)______ enough that Washington remained a marbled and remote figure. Indeed, by the 1920s Washington had become such (ii)______ personage that inevitably someone had to go to the other extreme and try to (iii)______ the legend.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, G　laudatory; a deified; debunk
uncover
uncover: standard unit headword
#220 Text Completion
The usual (i)______ spending public monies on scientific projects is that such projects have the potential to make our lives healthier, safer, and more productive. However, the fact that science—even "pure" science—can strengthen democracy and promote public participation in the political process is hardly ever (ii)______. It should be that scientific literacy (iii)______ democracy, and this is an important ancillary benefit of the promotion of science.The usual (i)______ spending public monies on scientific projects is that such projects have the potential to make our lives healthier, safer, and more productive. However, the fact that science—even "pure" science—can strengthen democracy and promote public participation in the political process is hardly ever (ii)______. It should be that scientific literacy (iii)______ democracy, and this is an important ancillary benefit of the promotion of science.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, E, H　rationale for; mentioned; energizes

Some fish farmers use upwards of five kilograms of wild fish as feed to grow one kilogram of carnivorous species like sea bass. Some fish farmers use upwards of five kilograms of wild fish as feed to grow one kilogram of carnivorous species like sea bass. #231 Text Completion
Environmental models—mathematical representations designed to simulate natural systems—are regularly used by litigants in legal disputes over environmental issues. Unfortunately, the (i)______ scientific model is (ii)______ in environmental tort litigation. Because of the adversarial nature of litigation, models are often used by one side to (iii)______ empirical evidence presented by the other. And because modeling is a particularly technical field, the task of assessing a given model’s relevance and reliability may exceed the abilities of judges and juries.Environmental models—mathematical representations designed to simulate natural systems—are regularly used by litigants in legal disputes over environmental issues. Unfortunately, the (i)______ scientific model is (ii)______ in environmental tort litigation. Because of the adversarial nature of litigation, models are often used by one side to (iii)______ empirical evidence presented by the other. And because modeling is a particularly technical field, the task of assessing a given model’s relevance and reliability may exceed the abilities of judges and juries.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, F, H　uncertainty inherent in; exacerbated; obfuscate

答案：B　extirpate

Such results have inspired thousands of people to (ii)______ in the hope of living longer, healthier lives. They have also led to a search for drugs that (iii)______ the effects of calorie restriction without the pain of actually going on a diet.Evidence has been accumulating since the 1930s that reducing an animals energy intake below its energy expenditure extends the life span and delays the (i)______ of age-related diseases in rats, dogs, fish, and monkeys. Such results have inspired thousands of people to (ii)______ in the hope of living longer, healthier lives. They have also led to a search for drugs that (iii)______ the effects of calorie restriction without the pain of actually going on a diet.

Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, E, H　onset; put up with constant hunger; mimic

Yet autobiography, even when ostensibly steeped in candor, tends toward (ii)______ through later perspectives, afterwords, and second thoughts, whereas letters have an undeniable (iii)______ , Offering select glimpses of the fraught and living moment.It is plausible to regard a collection of letters spanning youth and old age as (i)______ of autobiography: the precession of characters who inhabit a life and a chronology of incidents turn up reliably in either form. Yet autobiography, even when ostensibly steeped in candor, tends toward (ii)______ through later perspectives, afterwords, and second thoughts, whereas letters have an undeniable (iii)______ , Offering select glimpses of the fraught and living moment.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, E, H　an approximation; reconsideration; immediacy

Laws protecting intellectual property are intended to stimulate creativity, yet some forms of creative work have never enjoyed legal protection—a situation that ought to be of great interest. If we see certain forms of creative endeavor (i)______ as a result of uncontrolled copying, we might decide to (ii)______ intellectual property law. Conversely, if unprotected creative work (iii)______ in the absence of legal rules against copying, we would do well to know how such flourishing is sustained.Laws protecting intellectual property are intended to stimulate creativity, yet some forms of creative work have never enjoyed legal protection—a situation that ought to be of great interest. If we see certain forms of creative endeavor (i)______ as a result of uncontrolled copying, we might decide to (ii)______ intellectual property law. Conversely, if unprotected creative work (iii)______ in the absence of legal rules against copying, we would do well to know how such flourishing is sustained.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, E, H　languishing; extend; manages to thrive

To the avid reader of E. O Wilson, much of his most recent book Consilience: The Unity of Knowledge will be (i)______, as the book represents the culmination of a life spent thinking about everything from the social lives of ants to the social lives of people. Nonetheless, new thoughts have been mixed in with the old to produce a book remarkable for its (ii)______ and ambition.To the avid reader of E. O Wilson, much of his most recent book Consilience: The Unity of Knowledge will be (i)______, as the book represents the culmination of a life spent thinking about everything from the social lives of ants to the social lives of people. Blank 2

Sometimes people (ii)______ their errors; those same reviewers today, comparing the predictions with actual events, acknowledge how (iii)______ the author was.Twenty years ago, when the book was first published, most reviewers dismissed the author’s predictions as (i)______. Sometimes people (ii)______ their errors; those same reviewers today, comparing the predictions with actual events, acknowledge how (iii)______ the author was.

#258 Text Completion
One view of historicism holds that systems of belief prevalent during different period in history are ______ and therefore cannot be understood except in their own term.One view of historicism holds that systems of belief prevalent during different period in history are ______ and therefore cannot be understood except in their own term.
答案：B　incommensurable
anachronistic
anachronistic: standard unit headword
#259 Text Completion
Unlike most other serious journals, which drain money from their owners, the Review has long been (i)______. But the formula is not without its imperfections, which have grown more pronounced in recent years. The publication has always been erudite and (ii)______ but not always lively and readable. (iii)______, accompanied by a certain aversion to risk taking, has pervaded its pages for a long time.Unlike most other serious journals, which drain money from their owners, the Review has long been (i)______. But the formula is not without its imperfections, which have grown more pronounced in recent years. The publication has always been erudite and (ii)______ but not always lively and readable. (iii)______, accompanied by a certain aversion to risk taking, has pervaded its pages for a long time.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, D, I　lucrative; authoritative; A staleness
trendy
trendy: standard unit headword
#260 Text Completion
For almost two centuries, the German island of Sylt has offered various therapies for every conceivable (i)______, from broken bones to liver complaints. The local mud, saltwater, thermal pools, and spas have been deemed (ii)______ by the German medical system, which (iii)______ some of these treatments. Consequently, these treatments are widely used.For almost two centuries, the German island of Sylt has offered various therapies for every conceivable (i)______, from broken bones to liver complaints. The local mud, saltwater, thermal pools, and spas have been deemed (ii)______ by the German medical system, which (iii)______ some of these treatments. Consequently, these treatments are widely used.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, D, I　malady; healthful; funds

Here the review’s task is more (ii)______: the author’s useful and thought-provoking observations need to be (iii)______ the useless and tedious.Both very good and very bad books are easy to review. Praise and (i)______ come easily. But what of books that contain a muddle of virtues and vices? Here the review’s task is more (ii)______: the author’s useful and thought-provoking observations need to be (iii)______ the useless and tedious.

Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, E, H　censure; demanding; sifted from

#265 Text Completion
Campus-wide discussion on academic integrity can be (i)______ by the fact that faculty and students tend to define cheating in (ii)______ ways. Even when they concur on what cheating means, faculty and students often assign different levels of severity to specific violations. These differences can serve as a major (iii)______ the creation of a commonly accepted set of standards of integrity that are consistently applied to all academic work within the campus community.Campus-wide discussion on academic integrity can be (i)______ by the fact that faculty and students tend to define cheating in (ii)______ ways. Even when they concur on what cheating means, faculty and students often assign different levels of severity to specific violations. These differences can serve as a major (iii)______ the creation of a commonly accepted set of standards of integrity that are consistently applied to all academic work within the campus community.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, H　hampered; disparate; impediment to

Given our species’ increasing numbers and appetites which are reflected in and compounded by global climate change, even (i)______ species are likely to become endangered within the foreseeable future. If we are to avoid a calamitous loss of biodiversity, according to Golbe, we cannot simply let nature take its course. Ongoing conservation management is (ii)______, as we have, in a sense, become nature, and the responsibilities that come with the role cannot be (iii)______.Given our species’ increasing numbers and appetites which are reflected in and compounded by global climate change, even (i)______ species are likely to become endangered within the foreseeable future. If we are to avoid a calamitous loss of biodiversity, according to Golbe, we cannot simply let nature take its course. Ongoing conservation management is (ii)______, as we have, in a sense, become nature, and the responsibilities that come with the role cannot be (iii)______.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, E, G　now common; obligatory; jettisoned

Many popular musicians have (i)______ new digital technologies that allow them unprecedented control over their music. These musicians use computers to (ii)______ and modify their songs, resulting in a level of musical precision often unattainable naturally. Of course, though, as is often the case with new technologies, some traditionalists (iii)______ these developments.Many popular musicians have (i)______ new digital technologies that allow them unprecedented control over their music. These musicians use computers to (ii)______ and modify their songs, resulting in a level of musical precision often unattainable naturally. Of course, though, as is often the case with new technologies, some traditionalists (iii)______ these developments.

Blank 3
答案：A, F, G　incorporated; recast; balk at

Media reports regularly imply that industry support of scientific work is alone sufficient to (ii)______ that research. In adolescence, (i)______ interactions are crucial in forging a self-identity. To be sure, this process often plays out in (ii)______ as a means of defining and shoring up the sense of self. Kids will seek out like-minded companions, and spurn others who seem different. But when kept within reasonable bounds, this in-group (iii)______ generally evolves into a more mature friendship pattern.In adolescence, (i)______ interactions are crucial in forging a self-identity. To be sure, this process often plays out in (ii)______ as a means of defining and shoring up the sense of self. Kids will seek out like-minded companions, and spurn others who seem different. But when kept within reasonable bounds, this in-group (iii)______ generally evolves into a more mature friendship pattern.

Blank 3
答案：C, D, H　peer; cliquish social behavior; clustering

#284 Text Completion
Although the rich history of filmed versions of Shakespeare’s plays reaches back to the early twentieth century, filmmakers still appear to (i)______. They typically seek to (ii)______ their work by claiming that film techniques can improve upon Shakespeare’s text and on the conditions of theatrical performance, and yet such claims, asserted too strenuously, can seem defensive.Although the rich history of filmed versions of Shakespeare’s plays reaches back to the early twentieth century, filmmakers still appear to (i)______. They typically seek to (ii)______ their work by claiming that film techniques can improve upon Shakespeare’s text and on the conditions of theatrical performance, and yet such claims, asserted too strenuously, can seem defensive.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, E　doubt their own legitimacy; validate
